name: Fly Line
summary: A raised Cesium fly-line animation for single arcs, hub-spoke convergence, and bidirectional links.
useCases: Flight path and high-altitude route animation

lines.description: WGS84 route pairs. Each item is { from: { longitude, latitude, height? }, to: { longitude, latitude, height? } }.

mode.description: Fly-line behavior. single-arc renders each route once, hub-spoke is tuned for many routes converging to a center, and bidirectional mirrors every route.

color.description: CSS color used by base arcs, moving trails, and endpoints.

arcHeight.description: Approximate visible peak height of each raised arc in meters.

trailLength.description: Moving trail length as a fraction of total arc length.

pulseCount.description: Number of animated pulses per rendered route.

glowPower.description: Glow strength passed to Cesium PolylineGlowMaterialProperty.

showEndpoints.description: Show point markers for unique route endpoints.

update.code:
flyLine.update({
  mode: 'hub-spoke',
  color: '#ff58c8',
  speed: 1.8,
  width: 7,
  arcHeight: 52000,
  pulseCount: 5,
})

notes: Pass an existing Cesium Viewer; the SDK does not create or own the viewer.
The effect is built from Cesium PolylineGlowMaterialProperty arcs plus callback-driven moving trails.
Changing lines, mode, or arcHeight rebuilds route entities; style and endpoint visibility update live.
bidirectional mode mirrors each route internally, so callers only need to pass one direction.
Call destroy() during page cleanup to remove the data source and render loop.
